title: Module 1
content:
Introduction: preparation of polymer blends, thermodynamic criteria for
polymer miscibility, specific interactions, copolymer effect, phase separation,
spinodal decomposition, nucleation and growth, phase diagram, morphology,
blend characterisation techniques, Structure-property relationship, rubber
plastic blends, phase morphology, properties of blends prepared by dynamic
vulcanization, technological application,

title: Module 2
content:
Thermoplastic styrene block copolymers: polyester thermoplastic
elastomers,
thermoplastic
polyurethane
elastomers,
basic
structure,
manufacture, morphology, commercial grades, applications, thermoplastic
1,2- polybutadiene, trans 1,4-polyisoprene, ionic thermoplastic elastomers,
silicone based thermoplastic
elastomers,
polyamide 1,2-elastomers.
Introduction to particulate and fibre filled composites, applications, function
of matrix, function of fibres, polymer-fibre interface, factors influencing the
performance of composite

title: Module 3
content:
Coupling agents, bonding agents’short fibre composites, continuous fibre
composites, analysis of long fibre composites, analysis of short fibre
composites, critical fibre length, rule of   mixtures preparation and properties
of glass fibre, carbon fibre and aramid fibre, polymer concrete, polymer
impregnated concrete

title: Module 4
content:
Polymeric binders for rocket propellants: composite manufacturing
techniques, hand lay-up, spray-up, compression moulding, vacuum bag
moulding, pressure bag moulding, filament winding, resin transfer moulding,
pultrusion, Reinforced Reaction Injection Moulding
Suggestion on Project Topics
1.
After completion of first module students are able to prepare blend by any one of the   methods
2.
After completion of second module students are able to collect the datas of miscible and immiscible
blend and familiarize different types of fibres used in composites.
3.
After completion of third module students are able to produce polymer concrete.
4.
After completion of fourth module students are able to prepare one FRP product by hand lay-up
process.
